questions.q: What was the prophecy made by the astrologer about the King's death in 'The Tiger King'?
questions.answer: The astrologer had prophesied that the King would die exactly a hundred years after his birth, by the bite of a tiger.

questions.q: Which of the following options best describes the character of the Maharaja in 'The Tiger King'?
Fill in the blank: The Maharaja was determined to shoot _______ tigers in order to disprove the astrologer's prophecy.
What lesson does the story 'The Tiger King' teach us about the inevitability of fate?
questions.answer: The story teaches us that no matter how hard we try to change our fate, it will ultimately catch up with us. The Maharaja's attempts to kill a hundred tigers and avoid the prophecy only led to his downfall.

questions.q: True or False: The Maharaja in 'The Tiger King' was a just and fair ruler.
What was the significance of the tiger's bite on the Maharaja's finger in the story 'The Tiger King'?
questions.answer: The tiger's bite on the Maharaja's finger signified the fulfillment of the astrologer's prophecy and marked the end of the Maharaja's reign.

questions.q: Which of the following options is NOT a characteristic of the Maharaja in 'The Tiger King'?
Describe the relationship between the Maharaja and his aunt in 'The Tiger King'.
questions.answer: The Maharaja's aunt was a wise and kind woman who advised him to give up hunting tigers. However, the Maharaja did not heed her advice and continued to pursue his goal of killing a hundred tigers.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Maharaja's _______ nature ultimately led to his downfall in 'The Tiger King'.
What message do you think the author is trying to convey through the story 'The Tiger King'?
questions.answer: The author is trying to convey the message that one should not try to defy fate or challenge the natural order of things. The story also highlights the importance of humility and the dangers of pride and arrogance.
